title: Boulders and Buds
summary:
We were hoping to spend a few days climbing in the Flagstaff area, so I reached out to my high school friend Steven, who lives in town. He suggested climbing at The Peaks crag, which ended up being an awesome suggestion. The crag was a volcanic rock called dacite, and the texture and style of the climbing was reminiscent of Kootenai Canyon back in Missoula. The next day, we met up with Steven and his girlfriend to do some bouldering at Priest Draw. The rocks at Priest Draw are weathered limestone boulders that form pocketed and dead horizontal roofs. Although the style was rather unique and tiring to climb on, we spent over 6 hours bouldering and chatting. It was a very nice day reconnecting with an old friend. On the way out of town, we took a shower at Steven's house before hitting the road for Sedona.

-Colin

photos.caption: Colin pretending to climb on a roof at Priest Draw.
